subject: [PATCH 3/4] exportfs: don't pass struct iattr to ->commit_blocks

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever disagreed with Jeff Layton's suggestion to fold the first patch into this one, arguing that it requires its own patch description and motivation.
reviews.raw_body:
Disagree: I think the change in the first patch needs its own
patch description and motivation.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged Chuck Lever's suggestion to fold the first patch into this one, agreeing that it is unnecessary if this patch is taken.
reviews.raw_body:
Your call, but note that the changes in the first patch are completely
removed by this one. There's really no need for that patch if you take
this one too.

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-03-31.patch_summary: This patch removes the passing of struct iattr to the ->commit_blocks function in exportfs, simplifying the code and making it more efficient.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Christoph Hellwig noted that the big fat comment left in the patch is unnecessary, as the first patch's purpose was only to document the change and its behavior is minimal.
reviews.raw_body:
The big fat comment is still left.  The first patch is mostly intended as
clearly documenting what we're doing.  There is no behavior except for
sampling the current time more often in it anyway.

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch removes the passing of struct iattr to the ->commit_blocks function in exportfs, simplifying the code and making it more efficient.
